notes: The skill provides a clear, specific placement rule (6-9 minute mark) that the baseline consistently violated — recommending placement as early as minutes 2-3 in eval 3.

The most discriminating expectation across evals was the 'conversational tone, not marketing copy' check — the baseline defaulted to feature-listing and stat-quoting patterns even when it removed the forbidden opener.

The baseline got visual demonstration right in eval 1 (via tips section) but failed to integrate visuals into the actual script — the skill-guided response scripted visual direction into the delivery.

All three baseline runs scored exactly 0.5 (3/6) — the baseline is competent at thematic relevance and CTAs but consistently misses placement precision, runtime targets, and craft-level conversational tone.

The skill's strongest contribution is the placement framework (back half, 6+ minutes) and the anti-pattern list ('This video is brought to you by' opener, script reading) — these are not intuitive defaults for a general-purpose model.

Eval 2 (ad rewrite) has one potentially weak assertion: 'clearly more entertaining than the original' is subjective and difficult to fail — even mediocre rewrites satisfy it. Consider replacing with a structural assertion.

The skill produces noticeably longer, more structured responses (avg ~3000 chars) vs. baseline (avg ~1600 chars) — the extra length reflects genuine content (scripts, placement tables, checklists) rather than padding.
